revision.comment: Adding new NES game to the site.
revision.text:
{{Game |Guid=12340e81-8fd3-4d6d-a108-396b8f732a99 |Name=The Legend of Prince Valiant |Aliases=Little Lancelot |Image=Legendofprincevaliantbox.jpg |Deck=A side-scrolling action game based on the medieval comic strip about a bowl-headed adventurer. |ReleaseDateType=Full |ReleaseDate=1992 |Concepts=Concepts/Games Based on Comic Books, Concepts/Tie In, Concepts/Pit |Developers=Companies/Ocean Software Ltd |Genres=Genres/Action, Genres/Brawler |Locations=Locations/England |Platforms=Platforms/Nintendo Entertainment System |Publishers=Companies/Ocean Software Ltd, Companies/Piko Interactive LLC |Themes=Themes/Medieval, Themes/Fantasy }} == Overview == The Legend of Prince Valiant is a PAL-only NES game released by Ocean Software in 1992 and based on the serial newspaper comic of the same name of a noble adventurer during the time of King Arthur and Camelot. It was later rereleased as Little Lancelot by Piko Interactive, sold via their site, many years later in 2019; this was also the game's North American debut.
The game has players make their way through various levels, either in a side-scrolling format or a first-person format, avoiding traps while defeating enemies in their path. The player has a health gauge they can restore but limited lives, and traps like pitfalls or the swamp are instant kills.

revision.text:
{{Game | Name=The Queen of Duellist Gaiden α+ | Guid=3030-41378 | Deck=The final game in the Queen of Duellist series. | ReleaseDate=1994-03-05 | Characters=Characters/Chizuru_Takamori,Characters/Elza_Kanagusuku,Characters/Emily_Barnett,Characters/Great_Takegami,Characters/Kiriko_Koumiyouji,Characters/Mai_Maino,Characters/Miyuki_Matsuda,Characters/Reika_Kanbe,Characters/Ryu_Hou,Characters/Strange_Gold | Concepts=Concepts/Destructible_Clothing | Developers=Companies/Agumix | Franchises=Franchises/The_Queen_of_Duellist | Genres=Genres/Fighting | Locations= | Objects= | Platforms=Platforms/FM_Towns,Platforms/NEC_PC_9801,Platforms/Sharp_X68000 | Publishers=Companies/Xing_Entertainment | Games=Games/Arcana_Heart | Themes=Themes/Adult | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/16/164924/2421529-qodap.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"2421529-qodap.jpg","path":"16\/164924\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_avatar,scale_avatar,scale_small,square_small,square_mini,scale_medium,screen_medium,scale_large,square_tiny,square_medium,scale_super,scale_tiny,screen_kubrick","caption":""},"background":[]}' /> [[Franchises/The_Queen_of_Duellist|The Queen of Duellist]] returns to FM Towns and Sharp X68000 for one last installment. This game was also released in a "LIGHT" version for those two systems, which removed the game's "Graphic Mode". The cast remains the same from [[Games/The_Queen_of_Duellist_Gaiden_a|The Queen of Duellist Gaiden α]].

revision.text:
{{Game | Name=Them Bombs! | Guid=3030-69048 | Deck=Them Bombs! | ReleaseDate=2017-05-01 | ReleaseDateType=Month | Characters= | Concepts= | Developers= | Franchises= | Genres=Genres/Puzzle,Genres/Simulation | Locations= | Objects= | Platforms=Platforms/Android,Platforms/iPhone,Platforms/Nintendo_Switch,Platforms/PC | Publishers= | Games=Games/Keep_Talking_and_Nobody_Explodes | Themes=Themes/Crime | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/18/187788/3117367-9819296615-heade.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3117367-9819296615-heade.jpg","path":"18\/187788\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_small,scale_medium,scale_tiny,square_avatar,screen_medium,square_medium,scale_small,square_mini,scale_large,scale_super,square_tiny,scale_avatar,screen_kubrick","caption":null},"background":[]}' />
=='''Overview'''==
{{GBFigure|src=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/42/424316/3115187-screenshot_1.png|full=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/42/424316/3115187-screenshot_1.png|align=right|width=480|caption=The "electric locks" module, one of 12 featured in the game}} Them Bombs is a 2D couch co-op game for 2-4 people, in which one of the players has to defuse a bomb by disarming all of its modules before the timer runs out. However, the player can only properly disarm a module if they are guided by another player(s), whose task is to read the instructions from the bomb defusal manual.
Them Bombs is inspired by a similar game of the same genre, Keep Talking and Nobody Explodes.
The game was initially released on mobile devices by Yellow Dot Games in 2017. As of today, it is available on PC (Steam), Android and iOS devices, and Nintendo Switch. It is later coming to PlayStation 4 and Xbox One consoles.
=='''The story and the objective'''== The story features a crazy villain named Dr TiNT, who places ticking bombs in random densely-populated areas (eg. a hospital, plane, shopping mall, etc.), and then sends a text message with the location of the bomb to a random person. That person, called Unlikely Hero, is the player. The person reading the defusal manual is called an Expert.
In order to defuse the bomb, the Unlikely Hero and the Expert have to cooperate. That, above all, means verbal communication, as the Expert needs to explain all the steps of solving a module to the Unlikely Hero, and the Unlikely Hero must carefully describe the modules, ask the right questions and correctly interpret the answers.
=='''The bomb'''== 3, 4 or 6 (out of 12) random modules can fit into one bomb, each of them varying in difficulty and type of puzzle to solve. All the modules must be disarmed before the bomb can be successfully defused. Trying to disarm a module without following the instructions results in time penalty, which brings the player closer to detonation.
The player can choose from 3 levels – Beginner, Normal, and Hard. The higher the difficulty level, the less time the player has to defuse the bomb, and the more modules there are to be disarmed. The game also includes Custom mode, which lets the player choose the number of modules, set the defusal time length or choose particular bomb modules.
=='''The manual'''== The manual contains a detailed description of every module and a list of appendices used for decoding pieces of information. The instructions include both written and visual clues, alongside with tables of reference. Players can adjust font size and choose to read in dark, grey or light modes.
The manual can either be accessed in-game, as one of the game modes, or printed out.

{{Game |Guid=37777fff-c724-4d77-97d9-37d2f4bb2e5f |Name=Tideward |Image=AppIcon-512tideward.png |Deck=An unhurried idle role-playing game native to every Apple device. |ReleaseDateType=Full }} Tideward is an idle role-playing game developed by Manu Games LLC for Apple platforms, including iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple TV, Apple Vision Pro, and Apple Watch. The game centers on tending a small coastal world across twenty-three trades, with progression continuing in real time and during offline periods of up to twenty-four hours. It synchronizes across devices through CloudKit and is built without advertising, tracking, or third-party SDKs. A free TestFlight alpha is available ahead of a planned U.S. App Store release in February 2027.
